literature_check_runs.explanation: TOP2A (proliferation marker) minus EPAS1/HIF2A (angiogenesis/VHL-loss marker) recapitulates the canonical ccRCC proliferative-vs-angiogenic axis from IMmotion151 and Beuselinck subtypes. It is not a named published two-gene signature, but structurally it collapses a well-established biological dichotomy into its two archetypal genes.

literature_check_runs.explanation: TOP2A marks the proliferative/cell-cycle axis and EPAS1 (HIF-2α) marks the angiogenic/hypoxia axis that define ccRCC's canonical dichotomy (e.g., IMmotion151 Angio vs Proliferative, Beuselinck ccrcc2 vs ccrcc4). The difference is a compact restatement of this proliferative-minus-angiogenic contrast, not a new biological signal.
